description: Overall CAGE ranking, sorted by CAGE_EM then BARTHAG as tiebreaker.
notes: 1 = best team in D1.

description: Team name.
ESPN internal team identifier.
notes: Use for joins with other pipeline CSVs.

description: Athletic conference affiliation.
Rank within conference by CAGE_EM. 1 = best team in that conference.
notes: NaN for independents with no conference.

description: Overall win-loss record (season).
Home win-loss record.
Away win-loss record.
CAGE Efficiency Margin — our AdjEM equivalent. Points per 100 possessions better than an average D1 team, adjusted for opponent quality and pace. Primary sort column.
notes: KenPom equivalent: AdjEM. Higher is better. A+: ≥25, A: 18–25, B+: 7–12, Bubble: 0–2.

description: CAGE Adjusted Offensive Rating — points scored per 100 possessions, adjusted for opponent defensive quality.
notes: KenPom equivalent: AdjO. League average ≈ 103.

description: CAGE Adjusted Defensive Rating — points allowed per 100 possessions, adjusted for opponent offensive quality. Lower is better.
notes: KenPom equivalent: AdjD. League average ≈ 103. Elite defenses: <92.

description: CAGE Adjusted Tempo — possessions per 40 minutes, pace-adjusted. Neither fast nor slow is inherently better.
notes: KenPom equivalent: AdjT. High = up-tempo, Low = slow and deliberate.

description: Probability of beating an average D1 team on a neutral court. Torvik's signature metric. Computed via Pythagorean expectation: AdjO^11.5 / (AdjO^11.5 + AdjD^11.5).
notes: Torvik equivalent: BARTHAG. Elite ≥0.90, Bubble ≈0.50, Bottom <0.20.

description: Wins Above Bubble — how many more wins this team earned vs what a bubble team (AdjEM=0) would have earned against the identical schedule.
notes: Torvik equivalent: WAB. Accounts for schedule difficulty. A 25-5 record vs cupcakes may have lower WAB than 20-10 vs elite schedule.

description: Pythagorean win percentage — expected win rate based on scoring efficiency, independent of actual results.
notes: High expected_win_pct with low actual_win_pct = unlucky team due for regression upward.

description: Actual season win percentage.
Luck score — actual win% minus Pythagorean win%. Positive = winning more games than efficiency predicts (lucky). Negative = unlucky.
notes: KenPom equivalent: Luck. Teams with high luck tend to regress toward their Pythagorean expectation. Used in RegressedEff ensemble model.

description: Strength of Schedule — average opponent adjusted net rating this team has faced. Positive = tougher than average schedule.
notes: KenPom equivalent: SOS. Contextualizes record and efficiency ratings.

description: Record vs Quad 1 opponents (opp net rating ≥ +8.0 — elite, top ~25% of D1).
notes: Most important quad for NCAA tournament seeding. Q1 wins are the gold standard.

description: Record vs Quad 2 opponents (opp net rating 0 to +8.0 — above average).
Record vs Quad 3 opponents (opp net rating -8 to 0 — below average).
notes: Losses here are mildly concerning. Expected wins for tournament-caliber teams.

description: Record vs Quad 4 opponents (opp net rating < -8.0 — weak bottom tier).
notes: Losses to Q4 opponents are bad losses and damage tournament resume significantly.

description: Win percentage in Quad 1 games specifically.
notes: Most predictive single quad stat for NCAA seeding. Elite teams maintain >0.50 Q1 record.

description: Best win quality — highest opponent adjusted net rating in a game this team won.
notes: Higher = better marquee win. Elite resume: best win net >15.

description: Number of losses to Quad 4 opponents (embarrassing losses).
notes: Each bad loss significantly hurts tournament seeding and resume score.

description: Master composite ranking score combining efficiency, win probability, defense, momentum, resume, and clutch performance. Our single best overall team quality number.
notes: 100 = historically elite (2012 Kentucky tier). 85+ = title contender. 70–85 = top-5 seed. 55–70 = tournament team. 40–55 = bubble. <40 = NIT.

description: Letter grade for adjusted efficiency margin. Calibrated to actual D1 distribution.
Quality-wins composite on 0–100 scale. Rewards Q1 wins, win rate vs good teams, road success. Penalizes bad losses. Our version of Torvik's resume metric.
notes: 50 = bubble-quality resume. >60 = comfortable. >75 = top-10 resume. Useful for committee-style seeding analysis.

description: Defensive composite — how completely this defense shuts down opponents. Combines opponent eFG%, defensive rebounding, and adjusted defensive rating.
notes: 50 = average. >75 = elite shutdown defense. Low-scoring game predictor.

description: Momentum Quality Rating — recent form weighted by opponent quality. Not just win streak: a 5-game win streak vs weak opponents scores lower than 3 wins vs top-25.
notes: 50 = neutral. >65 = meaningfully hot. >80 = surging through elite competition.

description: Close-game excellence — performance in games decided by ≤5 points, adjusted for luck in those games.
notes: 50 = average. >70 = consistently closes games. Important for single-elimination tournament prediction.

description: How predictable/reliable this team is game-to-game. Inverse of net efficiency and shooting variance.
notes: 100 = machine-like (Virginia under Bennett). 50 = average. <35 = boom-or-bust — dangerous to pick in tournament.

description: Offensive Identity Score — how cohesive and defined the offensive system is. High = team executes a clear, repeatable offensive scheme.
notes: High identity teams are more consistent under tournament pressure.

description: Overall tournament readiness composite — aggregates schedule toughness, recent form, neutral-court performance, and defensive profile.
notes: Specifically calibrated for single-elimination. Best single number for tournament bracket analysis.

description: Star Reliance Risk — how dependent this team is on a single player's performance. High = fragile if the star has an off night.
notes: 50 = balanced team. >70 = one player carries them — high variance in tournament. Best paired with consistency_score.

description: Tournament DNA Index — composite capturing historical behavioral markers that predict tournament success: road wins, close wins, elite opponent wins.
notes: The 'proven it matters' metric. A team with dna_score >70 has demonstrated performance specifically in tournament-like conditions.

description: Downside performance estimate — team's CAGE_EM minus 1.5 standard deviations. What they look like on a bad night (captures ~87% of outcomes).
notes: In AdjEM units. Negative floor_em means on a bad night this team plays below average D1 level. Critical tournament risk metric.

description: Upside performance estimate — team's CAGE_EM plus 1.5 standard deviations. Peak performance potential.
notes: The gap between floor_em and ceiling_em reflects variance/unpredictability. Wide gap = boom-or-bust team.

description: Directional momentum indicator comparing last 5 games vs last 10 games net efficiency.
notes: ↑↑ SURGE: L5 better by 5+ pts. ↑ UP: 2–5 pts. → FLAT: within ±2. ↓ DOWN: 2–5 worse. ↓↓ SLIDE: 5+ worse.

description: Numeric encoding of trend_arrow for sorting. +2=SURGE, +1=UP, 0=FLAT, -1=DOWN, -2=SLIDE.
notes: Use for sorting/filtering. trend_arrow is for display.

description: Offensive system classification based on pace, 3-point rate, and free throw generation.
notes: Useful for matchup analysis — some archetypes systematically exploit others.

description: Percentage point gap between home and road win rates. Large positive = crowd-dependent, vulnerable on neutral courts.
notes: Tournament teams play on neutral courts. High home_road_delta = red flag. Negative or near-zero = road warrior, tournament-ready.

description: Binary flag: 1 = this team is shooting unsustainably well from 3-point range and is statistically due for a shooting correction.
notes: Teams with regression_risk=1 should be faded slightly in ensemble models. Three-point shooting is the most volatile CBB stat.

description: Binary flag: 1 = star player reliance is at a dangerous level AND team has shown fragility when the star underperforms.
notes: Combined star_risk + fragility indicator. More aggressive flag than star_risk alone.

description: Recent 3-point percentage (L5) minus season 3-point percentage. Positive = currently running hot from three.
notes: >5 = potential regression candidate. Pairs with regression_risk flag.

description: Effective Field Goal percentage — accounts for 3-pointers being worth 50% more than 2-pointers. (FGM + 0.5*3PM) / FGA.
notes: Dean Oliver's #1 factor (weight: 40%). League average ≈ 50.5%. Elite offense: >55%.

description: Opponent Effective Field Goal percentage allowed — defensive eFG% suppression.
notes: Lower is better. Elite defense: <46%. Combine with suffocation score for full defensive picture.

description: Turnover percentage — turnovers per 100 possessions. Lower is better.
notes: Oliver's factor #2 (weight: 25%). League average ≈ 18%. Elite: <15%.

description: Offensive rebound percentage — share of available offensive rebounds captured.
notes: Dean Oliver's #3 factor (weight: 20%). High orb_pct = second-chance points machine.

description: Defensive rebound percentage — share of available defensive rebounds captured.
notes: Complement to orb_pct. Elite defense: drb_pct >75%.

description: Free Throw Rate — free throw attempts per field goal attempt. Measures ability to get to the line.
notes: Dean Oliver's #4 factor (weight: 15%). High ftr + high ft_pct = free point machine.

description: Three-Point Attempt Rate — share of field goal attempts from behind the arc.
notes: High three_par teams live and die by 3-point shooting variance.

description: Three-point field goal percentage (season).
notes: Most volatile major shooting stat. Use three_pct_gap to flag hot-shooting teams.

description: Free throw percentage.
notes: Matters most in close games and tournament play when fouls are more common.

description: Win percentage in games decided by ≤5 points.
notes: Core input to clutch_rating. High close_wpct teams overperform in tournament settings.

description: Net efficiency rating (ortg minus drtg) averaged over last 5 games. Best indicator of current form.
notes: Primary input to Momentum ensemble model. Recency-weighted.

description: Net efficiency rating averaged over last 10 games. Medium-term form baseline.
notes: Used with net_rtg_l5 to compute trend_arrow. More stable than L5, less stable than season avg.

description: Timestamp when this row was last computed.
